Package simulation

Class SimulationRunner

java.lang.Object
simulation.SimulationRunner
public final class SimulationRunner extends Object
Discrete-event simulation runner for UAV/WSN experiments.

Supports two execution modes:

  1. Sequential (run(List)) — runs each intelligence strategy one after another on a single thread. Used for headless / batch operation.
  2. Parallel (runInParallel(List, List, PassCompleteCallback)) — runs strategies concurrently on separate threads so they can be visualised side-by-side in the GUI.

Comparison methodology

Both modes use the same random seed for every pass so that sensor-node deployment positions and movement sequences are identical. Only the UAVIntelligence strategy differs between passes.

Typical usage

   SimulationRunner.fromScenario(new WSNDataCollectionScenario())
       .withSeed(17L)
       .run();

To run a single intelligence without a scenario: 

   new SimulationRunner(SimulationConfig.defaults())
       .withPlannerFactory(() -> new GaAcoPlanner(GaAcoConfig.defaults()))
       .run(List.of(new GaAcoIntelligence(config, GaAcoConfig.defaults())));

  • Constructor Details

    • SimulationRunner

      public SimulationRunner(SimulationConfig config)
      Creates a runner for the given field/drone configuration. Use withPlannerFactory(java.util.function.Supplier<algorithm.PathPlanner>) to set a planning algorithm, and fromScenario(simulation.SimulationScenario) to configure from a complete scenario.
      Parameters:
      config - simulation configuration; must not be null

  • Method Details

    • setLiveRange

      public SimulationRunner setLiveRange(int range)
      Overrides the drone scan range at runtime.

      The new range takes effect on the very next drone tick, allowing the GUI dashboard to adjust the scan radius while a simulation is running. Pass a value ≤ 0 to revert to the configured range.

      Parameters:
      range - scan radius in cells; ≤ 0 reverts to SimulationConfig.getDroneRange()
      Returns:
      this for chaining

    • fromScenario

      public static SimulationRunner fromScenario(SimulationScenario scenario)
      Creates a runner fully configured from a SimulationScenario.

      The scenario provides the SimulationConfig, the PathPlanner factory, the MovementStrategy, and the NodeDeploymentStrategy. 

         SimulationRunner.fromScenario(new WSNDataCollectionScenario())
       .withSeed(17L)
       .run();
      Parameters:
      scenario - scenario to run; must not be null
      Returns:
      a configured runner, ready for withSeed and execution

    • withSeed

      public SimulationRunner withSeed(long seed)
      Locks the random seed used for every pass so results are reproducible. Call before run(List) or runInParallel(java.util.List<simulation.UAVIntelligence>, java.util.List<ui.VisualizationListener>, simulation.SimulationRunner.PassCompleteCallback).
      Parameters:
      seed - any long value; all strategies will share this seed
      Returns:
      this for chaining

    • withPlanner

      public SimulationRunner withPlanner(PathPlanner planner)
      Overrides the path-planning algorithm used by the UAV.

      The supplied instance is reused across all passes. If the planner holds mutable state between calls to PathPlanner.planRoute(java.util.List<domain.SensorNode>), use withPlannerFactory(java.util.function.Supplier<algorithm.PathPlanner>) instead so each pass gets a fresh instance.

      Parameters:
      planner - planner to use; must not be null
      Returns:
      this for chaining

    • withPlannerFactory

      public SimulationRunner withPlannerFactory(Supplier<PathPlanner> factory)
      Overrides the path-planning algorithm with a factory called once per simulation pass. Use this when the planner has per-run state, or when running passes in parallel.
      Parameters:
      factory - supplier of fresh planner instances; must not be null
      Returns:
      this for chaining

    • withMovementStrategy

      public SimulationRunner withMovementStrategy(MovementStrategy strategy)
      Overrides the movement model used by all targets in the simulation.
      Parameters:
      strategy - movement strategy to use; must not be null
      Returns:
      this for chaining

    • withMovementStrategyFactory

      public SimulationRunner withMovementStrategyFactory(Supplier<MovementStrategy> factory)
      Overrides the movement model with a factory called once per simulation pass. Use this when the strategy carries per-run state.
      Parameters:
      factory - supplier of fresh strategy instances; must not be null
      Returns:
      this for chaining

    • withDeploymentStrategy

      public SimulationRunner withDeploymentStrategy(NodeDeploymentStrategy strategy)
      Overrides the node deployment strategy used at the start of each pass.
      Parameters:
      strategy - deployment strategy to use; must not be null
      Returns:
      this for chaining

    • setVisualizationListener

      public void setVisualizationListener(VisualizationListener listener)
      Registers a single VisualizationListener used by the sequential run(List) entry point. Pass null to remove the listener.

    • run

      public void run(List<UAVIntelligence> intelligences)
      Sequential run with an explicit list of intelligence strategies. Each strategy runs in order on the calling thread; all passes use the same random seed.
      Parameters:
      intelligences - ordered list of strategies to compare (≥ 1)

    • run

      public void run(SimulationScenario scenario)
      Runs all intelligences from the given scenario sequentially. Equivalent to run(scenario.getIntelligences()).
      Parameters:
      scenario - scenario whose intelligences to compare

    • runInParallel

      public void runInParallel(List<UAVIntelligence> intelligences, List<VisualizationListener> listeners, SimulationRunner.PassCompleteCallback onComplete)
      Parallel run with an explicit list of intelligence strategies.

      Each strategy runs on its own background thread; all passes share the same random seed so sensor deployment and movement sequences are identical across strategies. This method blocks until every thread finishes. 

         runner.runInParallel(
       List.of(new PatrolIntelligence(),
               new MyACOIntelligence(),
               new MyRLIntelligence()),
       List.of(gui.createListener(0),
               gui.createListener(1),
               gui.createListener(2)),
       gui::notifyPassComplete);
      Parameters:
      intelligences - ordered list of strategies to run (must not be empty)
      listeners - per-tick GUI callbacks, one per strategy; entries beyond the intelligence count are ignored; a null entry or a shorter list means headless for that pass
      onComplete - called (from the finishing thread) when each pass completes; may be null

    • runInParallel

      public void runInParallel(List<UAVIntelligence> intelligences, List<VisualizationListener> listeners, SimulationRunner.PassCompleteCallback onComplete, boolean syncMode)
      Parallel run with optional sync mode.

      When syncMode is true a CyclicBarrier is inserted after each pass's per-tick listener call so that all passes advance to the next tick together. This makes the side-by-side visualisation frame-for-frame comparable — useful for research studies where you want both strategies to be rendered at exactly the same logical time.

      Sync mode only activates when every pass has a non-null listener; if any pass is headless the barrier is skipped for that run.

      Parameters:
      intelligences - ordered list of strategies to run (must not be empty)
      listeners - per-tick GUI callbacks, one per strategy
      onComplete - called when each pass completes; may be null
      syncMode - true to lock-step all passes tick-by-tick

    • runInParallel

      public void runInParallel(SimulationScenario scenario, SimulationRunner.PassCompleteCallback onComplete)
      Headless parallel run of all intelligences from the given scenario. Equivalent to runInParallel(scenario.getIntelligences(), null, onComplete).
      Parameters:
      scenario - scenario whose intelligences to compare
      onComplete - called when each pass completes; may be null
